Mining Resource Priority Policy

This policy makes the mining critical path win local contention while keeping the system work-conserving. Idle CPU and I/O can still be used by dashboards and maintenance jobs, but when the pool, active node, or database need resources, they take priority.

Critical Path
paid blocks
Backend Model
single active node
Timer
5 minutes
Avoided Risk
stale work

Why

BlockDAG mining runs on roughly one-second block cadence. Any avoidable local latency in template selection, Stratum job broadcast, submit handling, database writes, or peer propagation can turn solved work into stale, duplicate, overdue, or non-paying blocks.

Earlier live testing showed active/active template competition could reduce miner-normalized accepted block output by about 11-13%. The correct production shape is one canonical template lane and aggressive validation before the pool resumes mining.

Policy

ClassExamplesRuntime Treatment
Active mining lanenodeHighest Docker CPU/blkio weight, highest process priority, best-effort I/O priority 0.
Pool and accountingasic-pool, postgres, Postgres worker processesHigh Docker CPU/blkio weight, elevated process priority, best-effort I/O priority 0.
Observability and supportGrafana, Prometheus, Loki, exporters, dashboard, release seeder, snapshots, browserLow CPU/IO weights or idle I/O so they yield under contention.

Implementation

ops/apply-mining-host-tuning.sh reads pool metrics to detect the selected backend. It then updates Docker CPU and block I/O weights, applies process nice/ionice values, writes cgroup v2 memory.low protection for the node, pool, and Postgres, and applies a node memory.high soft throttle when configured. The default BDAG_NODE_MEMORY_HIGH=auto reserves host headroom during catch-up so the node is reclaimed/throttled before it can drive the whole host into global OOM; it is intentionally not a hard memory.max kill limit. The helper also keeps selected host interfaces on fq_codel when tc is available. Docker does not provide a portable per-container network priority control in this release path; network protection is host qdisc tuning plus keeping mining-critical CPU and disk I/O scheduling ahead of dashboard and maintenance work. It intentionally does not restart mining services or ASICs.

bdag-mining-host-tuning.timer reapplies the policy every five minutes and after boot. /etc/default/bdag-mining-host-tuning records BDAG_PROJECT_ROOT so the installed script can read the active release root. Fresh release installs run scripts/install-mining-appliance-profile.sh in non-destructive mode by default, then install P2P/IPFS support timers and bdag-mining-host-tuning.timer. Compose defaults also put container /tmp and /var/tmp on tmpfs so temporary churn avoids the chain-data disk.
